content: AI notes: This transcript captures a reference check call with Rohit about Vishal, a former colleague from LiveSpace, followed by a discussion about Aptile's upcoming product launch. Reference Check on Vishal Background and Role at LiveSpace - Vishal was among the first four team members at LiveSpace - Worked at LiveSpace for 5-6 years (2015-2021) - Grew from software engineer to director of engineering - Headed the CMS and pricing engine for on-demand construction of home interiors - Managed complex technical systems independently without much support Working Style and Strengths - Described as reliable, dependable, and level-headed - Technically outstanding and thoughtful in decision-making - Calm demeanor, especially during conflicts or heated discussions - More prepared and thoughtful before communicating ideas - Works well with different personalities in cross-functional settings - Considered among the top 5 team members Rohit has worked with Areas for Development - Not naturally charismatic or outspoken in communication - Might face challenges in business model development and identifying margin opportunities - As a technical founder, may need complementary skills for business aspects - Would benefit from hiring team members with complementary business skills Aptile Product Launch Discussion Action Items - [ ] Rohit to prepare and send early access package including: - [ ] Rahul and Mohit to help promote Aptile on LinkedIn (they have 110K and 50-55K followers respectivel...

Received from Rahul - 2026-03-13: Building in Fintech - PFM with a great wedge. This is a short blurb: CA sees taxes, not investments.  Broker sees investments, not liabilities.  Bank sees balances, not goals. India's 2 crore ₹30L+ income households have three advisors and no one who owns the outcome. Command is that entity. A personal finance agent that unifies accounts, loans, taxes, and investments and converts the full picture into proactive, executable actions. Before you ask. India's Account Aggregator framework makes unified financial data real. AI makes continuous personalised optimisation possible. The financial rails are live. We're building above every bank, broker, and insurer in India, the default decision layer for the end customer, the operating system for personal finance. Ankur Garg (Ex- Great Learning, Capital One, Morgan Stanley), Varun Gupta (Ex- Embibe, Ola, Linkedin) ---

Background & Experience: - Previously at Microsoft in OfficeAI team, led voice experiences (grew to 6M users) - Led product work on Microsoft Designer team (AI-native design engine) - Worked at Prathyush as first PM (10th/11th employee) - Left in December to explore new opportunities
Market Strategy: - Targeting two segments: - Testing with both segments to identify common primitives - Sees opportunity in Canva’s current “bloated” state with AI features

Prep 2026-03-19T23:33:00.000+05:30: Confluence Labs (YC W26) | confluence.sh What they do AI research lab building foundation models optimised for learning efficiency in data-sparse domains — drug design, materials science, hardware engineering, physics discovery. Their thesis: modern ML only excels where you can collect massive training data. In domains where data is expensive or slow to generate (physical experiments), current AI fails. Confluence's approach: combine LLMs with discrete program synthesis to maximise what you can learn from a small number of experiments. Two-track strategy: 1. Hypothesis generation — AI that designs maximally informative experiments so each one teaches you the most 1. Data-efficient modelling — program synthesis + LLMs to make predictions with far fewer data points Technical milestone Came out of stealth with 97.9% on ARC-AGI-2 at $11.77/task — SOTA at launch (Feb 2026). ARC-AGI-2 is considered one of the hardest AI benchmarks in the world (tests novel reasoning from just a few examples). Approaching 100% on what was previously considered unsolvable. Open-sourced the solver. Backed by YC and Paul Graham personally. --- Founders 1. Brent Burdick (Co-Founder) — [LinkedIn not publicly indexed] 1. Niranjan Baskaran (Co-Founder) — linkedin.com/in/niranjan-baskaran-a906a5205 Both are extremely young (combined age ~43), no traditional industry pedigree — pure research talent profile. The Paul Graham backing is notable signal here.

Ashwin (ContextFort) <> Ashwin (Z47): Met Ashwin @ Contextfort. SMB just based off intrinsics. Should track to see how their thought process and customer backwardness plays out over the next 2-3 weeks. + High IQ + H&H. Also scheduled my call at 11pm Saturday “working all day round”. + Conviction + “I don’t see the reason to waste time at a job and not startup. Starting up is the only thing that makes sense to me” + Customer backward. But not a spike – spoke to customers with every idea, but only have 4-5 conversations. + Thoughtful approach to picking the right problem +/? Thesis – right no building in construction tech. a platform to help architects and GCs collaboreate better. Previously was focussed on security for AI, . i.e., prompt injection and so on. ? Very out of domain ? Need to get a read on resourcefulness – are they able to generate more conversation in a few weeks. ? team building +/? Refs – soft ref from Anand was good but he’s his uncle. Intro came from Anand (Uncle) Next steps: - Meet in the U - If this is not a conflict for Kanav, we can connect them for a chat – PD what do you think? Notes:: Cookr Wed, 13 Nov 24 About the founders • Prabhu is the cofounder and CEO • Has a background in technology, worked at Microsoft for many years • Built Xbox consumer business and Xbox Live platform • This is his second startup, first one was in healthcare but didn’t succeed due to COVID • Other cofounders have backgrounds in food, technology, and packaging What is the business • Category marketplace for clean food and food products • Connects consumers with home kitchens for food delivery • Focuses on health, organic, and gourmet food products • Aims to provide home-cooked style meals through technology Scale of the business • Year 1 (FY21) - 96L GMV, spent around 10 cr • Year 2 (FY22) - 9.4cr GMV, total burn 16-17cr • Year 3 (FY23 target) - 20cr+ GMV, 30% take rate so Net Revenue 46-47%, -45% Net EBITDA, Burn 68L/month • Current monthly sales around 1.3 crores Operations • Initially had their own fleet • Now moved to 3rd party fleets (Porter, Dunzo, Shadowfax) • Facing challenges with Dunzo exiting the market and Zomato Blinkit pulling out • Building their own 3PL now • 52,000 chefs registered and waiting to be onboarded • 1300-1400 active chefs currently Consumer Insights • Core consumers: 28-45 age group working couples, 65+ senior citizens • Offers subscription meal services (30-day lunch/dinner plans) • Provides party meals for small gatherings (15-20 guests) • Menu focuses on home-style food not typically available in restaurants • Allows customization of meals W...

Notes from Ambika Pande 2026-02-07T16:24:00.000+05:30: 1. Unfortunately haven’t really gone deep into the E-Rupi use cases so wont be too much of an “expert” here. But from what i understand, its not a hard to replicate product per se (you’d know this as well as me : public rails = limited infra moat) - so it really depends how cotodel is going after the market. If they’re trying to solve for “spend management” as a whole - then maybe could be the Ramp version of India, but the differentiation is definitely not going to come from E Rupi. 1. Another core problem (high level - this is just my understanding of how erupi works) is that the spend is constrained to merchant category. In India MCC code mapping is still a very real problem, every 3 months there used to be an issuing blowing up at Rzp regarding this. In fact - a friend recently went to sell a suite of products to Kotak, Kotak was the most interested in a MCC predictor product, since that’s what they still have a lot of problems in 1. Another point is merchant acceptance - my hunch is if it’s another method, the acquiring bank / PSP needs to also build that acceptance and integrations, how scaled up that is and if everyone offers - that’s something I’m assuming you’d know better than me :) but haven’t heard about it being a “big bet” anywhere or serious buzz created around it, so it’s either a small effort (which is a good thing) or not a focus area at all

Product Overview: - Computer vision-based quality control system for textile knitting machines - System includes hardware box and user interface installed on each knitting machine - Detects fabric defects in real-time during production process - Reduces fabric wastage from ~1.5% to 0.1% through early detection - Currently covers 80% of common fabric types - False positive rate: 1-2 per day per machine - Each unit has 2 cameras for different angle detection
Market Opportunity: - Global market: 300,000 knitting machines - India market: 50,000 machines - Annual new machine sales: - Current focus markets: - Estimated global waste: $18B worth of fabric lost annually
Go-to-Market Strategy: - Two-pronged approach: - Currently prioritizing brownfield installations for next 6 months - Building brand recognition through “Nitai fabrics” certification
Business Traction & Financials: - Revenue: 7 CR expected by March 2024 - Current customers: 5 clients across India, Turkey, Uzbekistan, Bangladesh - Sold approximately 100 units to date - Unit economics: - First major customer (RT International):
